item.title: Fix stack base ptr that has multiple AllocIds
item.body: Fix https://github.com/KMiri-rs/KMiri/issues/19 by restoring stack pointer when  recorded sp is lower than current sp. (See https://github.com/KMiri-rs/miri/pull/6/changes/10762788860597f8cb53252d88e4e264187e1009 )

repo.name: miri
repo.description: [A forked miri for OS] An interpreter for Rust's mid-level intermediate representation

repo.name: miri
repo.description: [A forked miri for OS] An interpreter for Rust's mid-level intermediate representation

issue.title: Fix stack base ptr that has multiple AllocIds
issue.body: Fix https://github.com/KMiri-rs/KMiri/issues/19 by restoring stack pointer when  recorded sp is lower than current sp. (See https://github.com/KMiri-rs/miri/pull/6/changes/10762788860597f8cb53252d88e4e264187e1009 )

files.patch:
@@ -28,6 +28,7 @@ chrono-tz = "0.10"
 directories = "6"
 bitflags = "2.6"
 serde_json = { version = "1.0", optional = true }
+owo-colors = "4.3.0"
 
 [target.'cfg(unix)'.dependencies]
 libc = "0.2"

commit.message:
kmiri: print min_allocated_stack_addr and next_stack_addr

We can see the bug is min_allocated_stack_addr remains while next_stack_addr varies,
and stack variables are lower than next stack, meaning stack has been overridden and
caused the double free via base addr.

min_allocated_stack_addr=None next_stack_addr=ffffffff80fffff8
min_allocated_stack_addr=Some("[0xfffff0, 0xfffff8) size= 8B alloc87") next_stack_addr=ffffffff80fffff0
min_allocated_stack_addr=Some("[0xffffe8, 0xfffff0) size= 8B alloc88") next_stack_addr=ffffffff80ffffe0
min_allocated_stack_addr=Some("[0xffffd0, 0xffffe0) size=16B alloc89") next_stack_addr=ffffffff80ffffc8
min_allocated_stack_addr=Some("[0xffffb0, 0xffffc8) size=24B alloc90") next_stack_addr=ffffffff80ffffc0
min_allocated_stack_addr=Some("[0xffffb0, 0xffffc8) size=24B alloc90") next_stack_addr=ffffffff80ffffc0
min_allocated_stack_addr=Some("[0xffffb0, 0xffffc8) size=24B alloc90") next_stack_addr=ffffffff80ffffc0
min_allocated_stack_addr=Some("[0xffffb0, 0xffffc8) size=24B alloc90") next_stack_addr=ffffffff80ffffc0
min_allocated_stack_addr=Some("[0xffffb8, 0xffffc0) size= 8B alloc95") next_stack_addr=ffffffff80ffffb0
min_allocated_stack_addr=Some("[0xffffa0, 0xffffb0) size=16B alloc96") next_stack_addr=ffffffff80ffffa0
min_allocated_stack_addr=Some("[0xffff90, 0xffffa0) size=16B alloc97") next_stack_addr=ffffffff80ffff90
min_allocated_stack_addr=Some("[0xffff80, 0xffff90) size=16B alloc98") next_stack_addr=ffffffff80ffffc0
min_allocated_stack_addr=Some("[0xffff80, 0xffff90) size=16B alloc98") next_stack_addr=ffffffff80ffffc0

min_allocated_stack_addr=Some("[0xffff80, 0xffff90) size=16B alloc98") next_stack_addr=ffffffff80ffffa0
min_allocated_stack_addr=Some("[0xffff80, 0xffff90) size=16B alloc98") next_stack_addr=ffffffff80ffff90
min_allocated_stack_addr=Some("[0xffff80, 0xffff90) size=16B alloc98") next_stack_addr=ffffffff80ffffc0

min_allocated_stack_addr=Some("[0xffff80, 0xffff90) size=16B alloc98") next_stack_addr=ffffffff80ffff98

thread 'rustc' (48341) panicked at src/alloc_addresses/mod.rs:841:9:
assertion `left == right` failed
  left: (16777104, alloc98)
 right: (16777104, alloc114)

kmiri: a working version to compile miri_print

fix the https://github.com/KMiri-rs/KMiri/issues/19
by setting the stack pointer lower than allocated stack ptrs
kmiri: remove or comment all log!

kmiri: restore sp in stack popping only when it's lower than current sp

This fixes https://github.com/KMiri-rs/KMiri/issues/19
kmiri: remove min sp check from addr_from_alloc_id_uncached
